chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-888 of 2026 [Muhammad Haneef Chandio v. Province of Sindh & others] Present: Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. Riazat Ali Sahar J. 1. For orders on M.A. No.3650/2026. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on M.A. No.3651/2026. 4. For hearing of main case. 12.05.2026 Ms. Naina Raj Naurang, Advocate for petitioner. == 1. Urgency granted. 3. Exemption application is granted subject to all just exceptions. 2&4. The petitioner prays for quashing FIR No.195 of 2026 lodged at PS Badin for offences under sections 440, 147, 148, 149 and 504 PPC. The petitioner has been implicated as an accused person along-with others in said FIR. Per learned counsel the FIR is false and the police is not registering an FIR for the petitioner’s counter-version. The fact of the matter remains that the FIR sought to be quashed is still under investigation. The petitioner’s counter-version, if any, can be recorded by I.O of the case under section 161 Cr.P.C. Thereafter, as and when a challan is filed, the petitioner will have an alternate remedy before the Trial Court for premature acquittal under section 249-A or 265-K Cr.P.C, as the case may be. Presently, we are not inclined to entertain a prayer for quashment when investigation is on-going. Therefore, we dispose of this petition leaving the petitioner to aforesaid alternate remedy, however, with a direction to the I.O of the case to conduct a proper investigation. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish
